title: Sting 3.0 Tour
location: Synovus Bank Amphitheater at Chastain Park, Atlanta
description: Legendary musician Sting brings his 3.0 Tour to Atlanta's premier amphitheater. Expect hits spanning The Police solo career. Elegant outdoor venue, great for a sophisticated date night under the stars.

title: Breakaway Music Festival
description: Headlined by John Summit, Kaskade, FISHER, and Mau P. Two-day EDM festival — huge production, light shows, food trucks. Wear comfortable shoes and dance all night. Great festival-couple energy.

title: Euro Atlanta: I Love Reggae Night
location: Euro Atlanta, 1265 Powers Ferry Rd, Marietta
description: Reggae, Dancehall, Soca, Afrobeats with great drinks and oxtails that fall off the bone. Free entry and free parking. Casual, high-vibe atmosphere perfect for couples who just want to enjoy good music and good company.

title: Like Glue Fridays
location: Avenue Kitchen & Cocktail, 587 North Ave NW, Atlanta
price: Free before 11 PM with RSVP; $20 without
description: Atlanta's favorite weekly Afro-Caribbean party now in its 9th year. Reggae, Dancehall, Soca, Afrobeat & Top 40. Great food, great vibes, $20 onsite parking. A lively date-night option for couples who like to dance.

title: Soil Festival 2026
location: Maddox Park, 1115 Donald Lee Hollowell Pkwy NW, Atlanta
price: Tickets cover admission, food, beverages, workshops
description: Atlanta's premier urban agriculture event — locally sourced food, farmers market, composting workshops, beekeeping demos, kids zone, beer garden, yoga, and more. A wonderful wholesome date for food-and nature-loving couples.

title: Best of Atlanta Comedy Showcase
description: Top local comedians in one intimate show. Laughing Skull is one of Atlanta's best small comedy venues — the closer you are, the more you'll laugh. Great for couples who share a sense of humor and want a memorable night out.

title: The Poko Party
location: District Atlanta, 971 Marietta St NW, Atlanta
description: An all-female DJ lineup celebrating African rhythms — Afrobeats, Amapiano, Dancehall. District Atlanta has world-class production with 300 floating kinetic orbs and immersive LED walls. Cultured, high-energy, electric atmosphere for couples who love to dance.

title: Jim Breuer: Find The Funny
description: SNL legend and comedy veteran Jim Breuer brings his Find The Funny tour to Atlanta. Big-name comedian, great energy, memorable set. Solid date-night pick — laughs guaranteed.

title: Cocktails in the Garden
location: Atlanta Botanical Garden, 1345 Piedmont Ave NE, Atlanta
description: Adults-only evening in the garden with drinks, music, and beautiful scenery. Rotating food and cocktail vendors. Relaxed, romantic atmosphere — one of the most underrated date nights in Atlanta.

title: Like Glue Fridays
location: Avenue Kitchen & Cocktail, 587 North Ave NW, Atlanta
price: Free before 11 PM with RSVP; $20 without
description: Weekly Afro-Caribbean party every Friday. Reggae, Dancehall, Soca, Afrobeat & Top 40. Great food, great vibes. $20 onsite parking. Perfect for couples who want a fun night out without breaking the bank.

title: Salsa ATL: Tropical Elegance Saturday Latin Night
location: Brimstone Restaurant & Tavern, 10595 Old Alabama Rd Connector, Alpharetta
description: Weekly Saturday Latin night with Salsa & Bachata lesson at 8:30 PM, then open dancing until 2 AM. Food, drinks, free parking. Great for beginners and experienced dancers alike. Holding someone on a dance floor is a natural date ice-breaker.

title: Reggae Fest Massive ATL
description: Featuring Mavado, Busy Signal, Beenie Man, Spice, Elephant Man, Ding Dong, Vanessa Bling, Serani, Tony Matterhorn & T.O.K. — major reggae/dancehall legends on one stage. World-class production at Atlanta's top arena. The must-see show of the season for reggae fans.

title: R.A.S Memorial Weekend: 100% Reggae Afrobeats Soca
description: A memorial weekend celebration dedicated to reggae, Afrobeats, and Soca culture. Rhythm, community, and a great time for couples who love Caribbean and African music. Check listing for specific venue.

title: Atlanta Jazz Festival - Memorial Day Weekend
location: Piedmont Park and surrounding venues, Atlanta
description: Atlanta's premier free jazz festival celebrating its 49th year with Grammy-stacked lineups. Perfect for a laid-back daytime date — bring a blanket, pack a picnic, and enjoy world-class music in the park.

title: Stormwater Studios May Exhibitions
description: Open gallery with rotating exhibitions. May features Draw, Paint, and Connect group workshops. Cool, quiet, air-conditioned space — great for a cultured, low-key date. Browse art, talk about what you see, grab coffee after.

title: Saturday Farmers Market at Atlantic Station
description: One of Atlanta's best weekly farmers markets with local produce, artisan goods, food vendors, and plants. Free to browse, great people-watching. Start the morning together, grab coffee, then plan the rest of your date.
